recalls.Description: DriBiz is recalling its Ammo Tabs, which are rectangular, off-white fiber desiccant tiles measuring approximately 2 inches by 4 inches and 2.5mm thick. These tiles are designed to be placed in ammunition cans and pistol cases to absorb moisture and prevent humidity damage. The model number AMT-010 can be found on the packaging. Consumers should be aware that these tiles may not effectively protect firearms and ammunition from humidity, which could lead to corrosion or rust, increasing the risk of malfunction and potential injury.
recalls.Title: DriBiz Recalls Ammo Tabs Desiccant Tiles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact DriBiz at 877-896-7238 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at info@dribiz.com, or visit https://www.norustzone.com. Additional recall details are available at https://www.norustzone.com/recall by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Ammo Tabs Fiber Desiccant Tiles for Firearms and Ammunition

Images.Caption: Recalled AMMO TAB desiccant tile

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from July 2025 to December 2025, priced between $10 and $13.

Hazards.Name: Risk of corrosion or rust due to ineffective humidity protection leading to potential injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled Ammo Tabs and visit https://www.norustzone.com for instructions on obtaining a full refund and proper disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: DriBiz recalls Ammo Tabs desiccant tiles due to injury risks. Learn about the recall and how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: StopBox USA has issued a recall for specific AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro firearm locks that were shipped between June 2 and July 15, 2025. These locks are intended to secure AR-15 style rifles by preventing them from firing. The affected products are made from anodized aluminum and come in red and black, featuring five buttons for each finger and the StopBox 'S' logo along with 'CHAMBER LOCK PRO AR-15' etched on the back. Consumers can verify their shipping dates by reaching out to StopBox through various contact methods.
recalls.Title: Recall of AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro Firearm Locks by StopBox USA Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact StopBox USA at 877-919-4637, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email support@stopboxusa.com or visit https://stopboxusa.com/pages/stopbox-ar-15-chamber-lock-recall for more details.

Products.Name: AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro Firearm Locks

Images.Caption: Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro (Red)
Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro (Red)
Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro Inserted in a Firearm (Black)

Manufacturers.Name: StopBox USA, of Spokane Valley, Washington

Retailers.Name: Available at StopBox USA locations and online, as well as through various dealers nationwide from June to July 2025, priced around $150.

Hazards.Name: Manufacturing defect may allow the locks to be removed, risking unauthorized access to the firearm and potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled locks and secure their firearms through alternative means. They should contact StopBox USA for a prepaid return shipping label and instructions to return the locks for a full refund or a replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: StopBox USA recalls AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro firearm locks due to safety risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description:
Holosun Technologies has announced a recall of two models of Firearm Dot Sights: the HS503G-ACSS Red Dot Sight and the HE512C-GD Gold Dot Sight. These sights, designed to enhance firearm accuracy with an illuminated aiming dot, are packaged with a CR2032 lithium coin battery. The products are identifiable by their model names displayed on the packaging and product panels. However, the battery is not child-resistant, posing a serious ingestion hazard, especially for young children.

Consumers are urged to stop using the affected dot sights immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Holosun Technologies is providing a free recall kit that includes a compliant battery compartment enclosure and updated instruction manuals for the affected models. The company is also reaching out to all known purchasers to ensure safety compliance.
recalls.Title: Holosun Technologies Issues Recall for Firearm Dot Sights Due to Battery Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Holosun Technologies at 888-869-1180 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at cpscinquiries@holosun.com. Additional information is available online at https://holosun.com/media/news-events-171.html or by visiting www.holosun.com and selecting "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight Packaging, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight Top View, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight Packaging, Model HE512C-GD
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight, Model HE512C-GD
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight, Model HE512C-GD

Retailers.Name: Available at Primary Arms, EuroOptic, GRITR Sports, Davidson's, MidwayUSA, Carolina Sporting Arms, Palmetto State Armory, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at opticsplanet.com from February 2023 through August 2024, priced between $250 and $350.

Importers.Name: Holosun Technologies Inc., of City of Industry, California

Hazards.Name: The Holosun Red and Gold Firearm Dot Sights do not comply with federal safety regulations regarding coin batteries, as the lithium coin battery is easily accessible. This presents a risk of ingestion, which can lead to severe injuries, internal burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled Firearm Dot Sights and contact Holosun for a free recall kit that includes a compliant battery compartment enclosure and updated manuals. Holosun will also provide free return shipping for HE512C-GD models to install a compliant battery compartment tray.

recalls.metaDescription: Holosun recalls HS503G-ACSS and HE512C-GD Firearm Dot Sights due to battery ingestion hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: SIG SAUER is recalling its ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights, specifically Models SOR52001 and 7400579, which may pose an ingestion hazard due to easily accessible button cell batteries. These sights are identifiable by the SIG SAUER logo and ROMEO5 name, with serial numbers ending in K or M. The model number is located on the packaging, and the sights are packaged with a CR2032 button cell battery in black color.
recalls.Title: SIG SAUER Recalls ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights Due to Ingestion Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Sig Sauer at 603-610-3000 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at ROMEO5Recall@sigsauer.com, or visit https://www.sigsauer.com/safety-center or https://www.sigsauer.com and click on 'Recall Notices' for more information.

Products.Name: SIG SAUER ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights

Images.Caption: Recalled SIG SAUER ROMEO5 Red Dot Firearm Sight
The product serial number is located on the bottom of the sight

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, Sportsman's Warehouse, Academy Sports, Scheels, and various independent firearms stores nationwide, as well as online at Sigsauer.com, Amazon.com, Opticsplanet.com, EuroOptic.com, Palmettostatearmory.com, Midwayusa.com, Chattanoogashooting.com, Gritrsports.com, Bereli.com, Battlehawkarmory.com and other online optics retailers from February 2023 through December 2024, priced between $130 and $200.

Importers.Name: Sig Sauer Inc., of Newington, New Hampshire

Hazards.Name: The firearm sights have button cell batteries that do not comply with federal safety regulations, creating an ingestion hazard due to easy access. The batteries lack child-resistant packaging as mandated by Reese's Law and do not carry necessary warnings. Ingesting button cell batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, or even death in children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled sights immediately and contact SIG SAUER for guidance on submitting a photo of the serial number to obtain a free recall kit. This kit includes a new cap with proper labeling and an updated instruction manual. Instructions for locating the serial number will be provided. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: SIG SAUER recalls ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights due to ingestion risks from accessible button cell batteries. Learn more about this safety recall.

recalls.Description:
Academy Sports + Outdoors has announced a recall for their Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes due to a significant safety concern. The 12-Gun Safe is identified by model number 179498, while the 18-Gun Safe carries model number 179499. Both safes feature 'Redfield' printed on the front door above the keypad, with the model name and serial number located on a red label at the bottom right corner of the door. The safes, designed for storing firearms and valuables, are dark gray steel constructions with specific dimensions for each model.

The 12-Gun Safe measures 53 inches in height, 15 inches in width, and 16 inches in depth, whereas the 18-Gun Safe is 55 inches high, 18 inches wide, and 18 inches deep. Certain serial numbers for both models are affected, posing a risk of unauthorized access due to potential mechanical failures in the locking mechanism.
recalls.Title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Issues Recall for Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes Due to Serious Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled safes immediately and return them to any Academy Sports + Outdoors location for a free replacement or refund. For further assistance, contact Academy Sports + Outdoors Customer Care at 888-922-2336 from 7 a.m. to 10 p.m. CT, email customerservice@academy.com, or visit www.academy.com/product-recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield 12-Gun Fireproof Safe
Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield 18-Gun Fireproof Safe
Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield Gun Fireproof Safe serial number location

Retailers.Name: Available at Academy Sports + Outdoors stores and online at academy.com from February 2024 through August 2024, priced between $350 and $600.

Importers.Name: Academy Ltd., d/b/a Academy Sports + Outdoors, of Katy, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to mechanical failure of internal locking plates

Remedies.Name: Return for free replacement or refund

recalls.metaDescription: Academy Sports + Outdoors recalls Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes due to serious safety risks. Return for a free replacement or refund.

recalls.Description: Bulldog Cases is recalling several models of their biometric gun safes, including the Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault, Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault with Shelf, and Magnum Biometric Top Load Pistol Vault. These black steel safes are designed for securing firearms and valuables. The dimensions of these models are as follows: the Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault (BD4030B) measures 11.5" x 8" x 5.5", the Vault with Shelf (BD4040B) measures 11.5" x 10" x 8", and the Top Load Vault (BD4055B) measures 11.5" x 9.8" x 2.5". Each model features the brand name 'Bulldog' prominently on the front, and the model number can be found on a silver sticker located at the back right corner of the safe.
recalls.Title: Bulldog Cases Recalls Biometric Gun Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Bulldog Cases at 888-799-8001 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at recall@bulldogcases.net or visit https://bulldogcases.com/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4030B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault with Shelf, Model No. BD4040B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Top Load Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4055B

Injuries.Name: The firm received four reports of the safe being opened by an unauthorized user. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Walmart, various firearm retailers nationwide, and online at www.Amazon.com from July 2016 through January 2024, priced between $194 and $216.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to serious injury or death

Remedies.Name: Stop using the biometric feature, remove batteries, and use the key for storage. Contact Bulldog for a repair kit or free replacement safe.

recalls.metaDescription: Bulldog Cases recalls biometric gun safes due to serious injury risks. Learn how to get a repair kit or replacement safe.

recalls.Description: Machir is recalling specific biometric personal safes designed for storing valuables. These safes measure approximately 10 inches by 14 inches by 10 inches, with serial numbers located on the lower right corner of the front door. The recall affects a range of serial numbers, including SW190035551 to SW190038050, SW190070151 to SW190072650, and several others listed in the original announcement.
recalls.Title: Machir Recalls Biometric Personal Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Machir at 888-505-5195 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at Info@machir.com, or visit https://machir.com/biorecall/ for more details. Alternatively, go to https://machir.com/ and click on 'Biometric Safe Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe with key inserted
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe packaging

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the recalled safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from September 2021 to February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to potential serious injury or death, especially if firearms are stored.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the biometric lock, remove the batteries, and utilize the key for access until a free replacement is obtained. Contact Machir for guidance on disabling the biometric lock and to arrange for a replacement safe. To receive a new safe, consumers will need to disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to Info@machir.com. Detailed instructions for safely destroying the biometric reader can be found at http://machir.com/biorecall/.

recalls.metaDescription: Machir recalls biometric personal safes due to serious injury risks. Learn how to disable the lock and get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: BBRKIN has issued a recall for its Biometric Gun Safe, model QCJJ01. This gray steel safe, designed for storing firearms and valuables, measures approximately 13 inches by 14 inches by 15 inches and includes one shelf, accommodating around 5 firearms. The brand name 'MouTec' is displayed on the front, and the serial number can be found on a label in the lower right corner. Affected serial numbers range from SQC202121000 to SQC202247603.
recalls.Title: BBRKIN Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BBRKIN via email at support@bbrkin.com or visit www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement. You can also access the recall form by going to www.securitysafe.store and clicking on 'Recall Form' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled BBRKIN Biometric Firearm Safe model number QCJJ01

Injuries.Name: The firm received one report of the safe opened by an unauthorized user, a 6-year-old boy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com between September 2021 and February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Importers.Name: Jomani International Inc., of Monterey Park, California

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Moyumaoyi Co. Ltd., dba BBRKIN, of China

Hazards.Name: Unauthorized access to the biometric lock may lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the biometric feature, remove the batteries, and rely on the key for firearm storage. Contact BBRKIN for a repair kit or a free replacement safe. To receive a replacement, consumers need to disable the biometric reader and email a photo of it to support@bbrkin.com. Detailed instructions for disabling the biometric reader can be found at www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement.

recalls.metaDescription: BBRKIN recalls Biometric Gun Safes due to serious safety risks. Contact them for a repair kit or free replacement.

recalls.Description: Awesafe has issued a recall for specific biometric gun safes that can accommodate two pistols. These safes are black and prominently feature the brand name 'Awesafe' on the front. The recall is due to a serious safety concern regarding the biometric lock, which may fail and allow unauthorized access, posing significant risks of injury or death.
recalls.Title: Awesafe Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Awesafe via email at Recall@awesafeus.com or visit their website at https://awesafeus.com/RECALL for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - closed
Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - open

Injuries.Name: The firm has received reports of 71 incidents of the recalled gun safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations nationwide and online through Amazon.com and Walmart.com from August 2019 to December 7, 2022, priced around $130.

Importers.Name: Shenghaina Technology Co. Ltd., d/b/a Awesafe, of China

Hazards.Name: Failure of the biometric lock allowing unauthorized access, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the biometric feature, remove the batteries, and utilize the key for safe storage until a free replacement is obtained. Awesafe will provide instructions for disabling the biometric feature and issuing a replacement safe. Users must disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to Recall@awesafeus.com to qualify for the replacement. Detailed instructions for safely disabling the biometric reader are available at http://awesafeus.com/RECALL. Once the new safe is received, the recalled unit should be disposed of according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Awesafe recalls biometric gun safes due to serious safety risks. Learn how to disable the feature and obtain a free replacement.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canisters, model number SDP-G-03. These canisters resemble a toy gun, featuring a black handle and an orange body. Only those units with an expiration date of 9/2027 are affected, which can be found on the white label located on the side of the canisters.
recalls.Title: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray Canisters Recalled for Emergency Failure
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Security Equipment Corp. at 800-325-9568 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or email customersupport@sabredred.com. More details are available online at www.saberered.com by clicking on "Aim & Fire Pepper Spray Recall" or visiting https://www.sabrered.com/2023-aim-and-fire-pepper-gel-recall/.

Products.Name: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canisters

Images.Caption: Recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canister
Red nozzle on top of the recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Canister

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the red nozzle missing. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Security Equipment Corporation, of Fenton, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Available at sporting goods stores across the country and online at Amazon.com between July 2023 and August 2023 for approximately $50.

Hazards.Name: The canisters lack the necessary red nozzle on the valve stem, preventing the deployment of pepper gel spray when triggered, which could leave individuals defenseless in emergencies.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled canisters immediately and reach out to Security Equipment Corporation for guidance on obtaining a free replacement. A free shipping label will be provided for returning the product.

recalls.metaDescription: SABRE recalls Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray canisters due to failure in emergencies. Contact for free replacement details.
